libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property is indicated. Tests should be pure in-memory Maven artifact/filter exercises. For Maven ProjectBuildingResult stubs on the 3.1.1/Maven 3.1.0 path, use org.eclipse.aether graph types; keep legacy org.sonatype.aether usage limited to the Sonatype transformer or explicit legacy fallback coverage.

deterministic_setup.reason: maven-common-artifact-filters 3.1.1 pulls Maven 3.1.0, whose old org.eclipse.sisu.plexus transitive path requests a missing sisu-guice 3.1.0 noaop artifact under Gradle; selecting a newer available Sisu Plexus avoids the unresolved transitive artifact before tests can compile.
The library exposes legacy Sonatype Aether transformer/fallback APIs as provided dependencies, so tests covering those public APIs need the API jar explicitly.
SonatypeAetherFilterTransformer public methods return Sonatype Aether util filter types that are provided by the library POM, not by the normal Gradle test scaffold.
The 3.1.1 POM declares Maven Resolver APIs as provided and the library exposes Eclipse/Maven Resolver transformer types.
EclipseAetherFilterTransformer public methods return Maven Resolver util filter types declared as provided by the library POM.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8666 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.shared:maven-common-artifact-filters:3.1.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The Sisu Plexus dependency is a classpath override to avoid an unavailable legacy classifier artifact; native-image verification remains authoritative for whether the override is acceptable.

Existing 3.0.0 tests that implement Maven ProjectBuildingResult with org.sonatype.aether types will still need source adaptation for Maven 3.1.0, whose DependencyResolutionResult uses org.eclipse.aether types.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The 3.1.1 artifact upgrades to Maven 3.1.0 and its normal Gradle test classpath cannot resolve cleanly without extra classpath preparation; public Aether transformer APIs also rely on provided dependencies.
